name: Path Traversal and LFI Detection Pack
description: Detects directory traversal attempts, encoded path manipulation, and local file inclusion payloads targeting application file systems.
objective: Identify and block requests attempting to traverse directories or include sensitive local files via request components.
risk_mitigated: Unauthorized file disclosure, source code exposure, credential leakage, and host compromise paths enabled by traversal/LFI payloads.

rules.description: Detect raw directory traversal sequences in URL, query string, body, or headers.
Detect URL-encoded and double-encoded traversal separators and dot segments.
Detect common LFI targets and sensitive local file references.
Detect PHP stream wrappers commonly used in LFI exploitation.

tuning_notes: Start in log/challenge mode to baseline legitimate traffic that includes relative paths.
Exclude known-safe endpoints that accept file path fragments (for example, developer tooling routes).
Normalize and decode inputs once in the WAF pipeline before evaluating encoded traversal signatures.
Pair with application allowlists for file identifiers rather than filesystem paths.
deployment_notes: Deploy as a high-priority custom rule set ahead of generic allow rules.
Enable blocking for high-confidence signatures (sensitive file targets and wrapper payloads) first.
Roll out raw/encoded traversal signatures in monitor mode for at least 7 days before full block in mixed legacy environments.

kpis: Traversal/LFI rule match volume by endpoint
Top offending source IPs and ASNs
alerts: Spike in encoded traversal matches over 3x baseline in 15 minutes
Any sustained hits against /etc/passwd, web.config, or .env indicators
Repeated wrapper payload attempts from a single source
